verdicts.note: On-page account by Sandor Clegane of Gregor's dubbing by Rhaegar at a tourney four years before AGOT; witnessed scene, chain-mail verified, knightly vows spoken, dubbing confirmed. Both nodes exist.
Barristan Selmy confirms to Ned Stark that Robert knighted Hugh of the Vale at King's Landing before riding north, in Jon Arryn's memory. Barristan as witness/informant. Both nodes exist.
Tyrion's account of Jacelyn Bywater's knighting by Robert at Pyke during Balon Greyjoy's Rebellion, confirmed as a known fact in Tyrion's discussion with Janos Slynt. Both nodes exist.

On-page Trial of Seven dubbing scene at Ashford: Lyonel Baratheon (the Laughing Storm) personally knights Raymun Fossoway, performing the full ceremony with sword and vows. Direct witness narration. Both nodes exist.
Rolly Duckfield's direct first-person account to Tyrion: 'Any knight can make a knight, and Griff made me.' Griff = Jon Connington. On-page dialogue. Both nodes exist.
Barristan Selmy's own POV narration of his life history: he received knighthood at the hands of Aegon the Unlikely (Aegon V) during his reign. First-hand account of personal event. Both nodes exist.
Littlefinger to Alayne/Sansa: 'Bronze Yohn knighted him' (Harrold Hardyng after winning the squires' melee at Runestone). Littlefinger as informant of known event. Both nodes exist.
Glendon Flowers' first-person account to Dunk: 'I was knighted by Ser Morgan Dunstable of Tumbler's Falls, two dozen people saw it.' Direct witness testimony. Both nodes exist.

Duncan the Tall's first-person account to tourney steward: 'He knighted me before he passed, with his own sword' (Ser Arlan of Pennytree). Direct first-hand account of personal dubbing event. Both nodes exist.

On-page narration by Ser Arys Oakheart: Quentyn Martell was fostered by Lord Yronwood and 'had even taken knighthood at his hands in preference to the Red Viper's.' On-page account of known biographical fact. Both nodes exist.
